title: glownote — Selection Toolbar / Popover Fix-It
environment: WXT build (chrome-mv3), Vitest jsdom (50 tests), chrome-devtools MCP real-browser repro (isolated context browser-campaigns)

sections.title: Root cause — mouseup re-show race (the 'not working' bug)
sections.context: In src/entrypoints/glownote.content/index.ts the document 'mousedown' handler guarded against events inside the toolbar via composedPath(), but the 'mouseup' handler did not. Clicking a toolbar color button therefore re-entered the mouseup path (selection still present) and called showSelectionToolbar() again — re-showing the toolbar on top of the popover that onHighlight had just opened. Net effect for the user: highlighting 'didn't really work' — the toolbar flickered back and sat over/instead of the note popover.
sections.output_sample:
mousedown handler: composedPath() guard present
mouseup handler: NO composedPath() guard -> re-shows toolbar on in-toolbar clicks

sections.title: Fix — composedPath() guard on mouseup + selection hardening
sections.context: Added isInsideGlowNoteUi(e) (checks composedPath for the glownote-selection-toolbar / glownote-popover shadow hosts) and an early return at the top of the mouseup handler (composedPath read synchronously before the setTimeout). Also added onmousedown={e=>e.preventDefault()} to the SelectionToolbar root so the page selection is reliably preserved when clicking a color (best-practice for selection toolbars). Human-on-the-loop design notes recorded in STATUS.
sections.output_sample:
if (isInsideGlowNoteUi(e)) return;  // top of mouseup handler
SelectionToolbar root: onmousedown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}

sections.title: Real-browser reproduction + verification of the fix
sections.context: Reproduced the exact scenario in the real Chromium (chrome-devtools MCP): a shadow-DOM host named glownote-selection-toolbar over a live text selection. A real mouse click on the toolbar button: onHighlight fired exactly once, the page selection was preserved ('voir dire process'), the FIXED composedPath guard suppressed the re-show (0 re-shows), while the OLD (unguarded) logic would have re-shown the toolbar (1 re-show). Confirms both the bug and the fix with real shadow DOM + composedPath + real click.
sections.output_sample:
highlightCalls: 1
selectionPreservedAtClick: "voir dire process"
reshowWithGuard_FIXED: 0
reshowNoGuard_OLDBUG: 1

sections.title: E2E pipeline proven by integration test (highlight -> note -> export)
sections.context: Added tests/integration/highlight-flow.test.ts wiring the REAL modules (css-highlight, anchoring, storage/db on fake-indexeddb, export/markdown) exactly as the content script does. 6 cases: create+render+persist highlight; attach note and export it; multi-highlight export sorted by document position; re-anchor a saved selector back to the same text; sub-selection dedupe detection; remove highlight clears render+storage+export. Full suite: 50/50 green (was 44).

sections.title: Environment blocker — true in-extension E2E not runnable here
sections.context: The chrome-devtools MCP browser is a puppeteer-managed Chromium launched with --disable-extensions and --remote-debugging-pipe (verified via chrome://version). An unpacked WXT build cannot be loaded into it, and the MCP cannot attach to a separately-launched Chrome. So the click-through was done against a faithful real-browser repro of the content-script logic (real shadow DOM/selection/Highlight API) rather than the packed extension. A follow-up to stand up a proper WXT-dev (web-ext) E2E harness is handed off (see PM queue / HANDOFF).
